title: Safety Program/Risk Management: Emergency Plans
description: Conroe ISD maintains emergency plans to help keep students and staff safe during crises such as severe weather, fires, or other threatening situations. The district coordinates these plans with local emergency management agencies to ensure a consistent and practiced response when emergencies occur. Parents and community members can feel confident that schools have documented procedures in place to protect everyone on campus.

timeline_local.summary: This update to CKC (Local Policy) restructures the Multihazard Emergency Operations Plan section by adding new language about the plan's required scope and the specific procedures it must address, while replacing the "Violent Activity" section heading and its associated paragraph with a broader "Threat to Health or Safety" framework that expands parent notification requirements beyond just violent activity.
changes_added: The District shall adopt and implement a multihazard emergency operations plan for use in the District facilities. The plan must address prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, reunification, and recovery in accordance with Texas law and regulation.
Multihazard (as underline/emphasis on the word in the heading 'Multihazard Emergency Operations Plan')
Threat to Health or Safety (replacing 'Violent Activity' in the section heading)

The Superintendent shall adopt procedures providing immediate notification to parents, guardians, and other persons standing in parental relation in circumstances involving a significant threat to the health or safety of students, including identification of the individual with responsibility for overseeing the notification.
changes_removed: As required by law, the emergency operations plan shall include the District's procedures addressing:
1. Reasonable security measures when District property is used as a polling place;
2. Response to an active shooter emergency;
3. Response to a nearby train derailment, as applicable; and
4. Access to campus buildings and materials necessary for a substitute teacher to carry out the duties of a District employee during an emergency or an emergency drill.
Violent Activity (from the section heading 'Notice Regarding Violent Activity')

The Superintendent shall develop procedures to notify parents regarding violent activity that has occurred or is being investigated at a campus or other District facility or at a District-sponsored activity.
timeline_local.notes: The document does not include a cover page title heading (e.g., "Discuss Revisions to..." or "Adopt Revisions to...") — the page shown appears to be the redline policy page itself, not a standard recommendation cover page. Action has been set to 'unknown' as a result. All red underlined text and red strikethrough text have been captured. The word 'Multihazard' in the heading appears underlined in red indicating it was added/emphasized. The four numbered list items under the emergency operations plan procedures are all struck through (removed).

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's CKC (Emergency Plans) local policy adds a new required item to the Emergency Operations Plan: provisions for ensuring the safety of students, staff, and spectators during extracurricular activities sponsored or sanctioned by the District.
changes_added: Provisions for ensuring the safety of students, staff, and spectators during extracurricular activities sponsored or sanctioned by the District;
timeline_local.notes: The cover page does not include a large bold action-determining title heading (e.g., "Discuss Revisions to..." or "Adopt Revisions to..."); the document simply presents the policy with Version 1 and a blank Date Issued field. Board action could not be determined from the cover page title, so 'unknown' was used. The red underlined item #3 is clearly a new addition. No red strikethrough text was present, so no removals were recorded. This appears to be an initial adoption (Version 1) of this local policy.

description_local: This policy covers a range of school district operations, including how board members request information, how the district purchases goods and services, emergency and safety planning, security personnel, and the management of school buildings and grounds. It affects school board members, district staff, and anyone who uses school facilities or depends on the district's safety and security systems. Parents and community members should be aware of these policies because they directly shape how safe and well-maintained schools are, how public funds are spent, and how the district prepares for and responds to emergencies.
